data.body:
COMPLETION-AWARE CONTAINER PLAN-REVIEW — brainstorm -> design -> decomposition -> signed plan-review PASS.

GOAL: turn placeholder epic 66ac (woozy-pea-kin) into a fully-defined, plan-reviewed, decomposable epic.

PROBLEM (refined during brainstorm): when the plan-review claim gate legitimately RE-FIRES on a
mid-flight container (epic reopened after a pause -> ADR 0009; or a material course-correction to the
epic plan), it reviews as if fresh and RE-LITIGATES acceptance criteria already delivered by
closed+attested children -> false BLOCKs + coaching to tighten shipped AC. The claim CASCADE is NOT a
driver (it only fires while the parent is `open`; an in_progress epic does not re-gate on further
child claims). Code-drift invalidation is INTENTIONAL and unchanged (a container's plan IS reviewed vs
codebase state — G6 runs at container scope): this epic changes only what a re-fired review DOES.

DECISION: option (b)/hybrid — completion-aware container review, keyed on VERIFIED delivery (the
signed completion attestation closed children already carry), never on bare `closed` status.

DESIGN (converged over ~10 turns, each grounded in prior art + adversarial subagent critique):
- Pass-1 stays completion-blind (full recall). A SEPARATE Pass-2 sub-call `plan_review_completion`
  (mirrors `plan_review_novelty`, passes.py:147) ALONE receives a delivered-children manifest and
  emits three atomic orthogonal sub-answers per finding, each a CLOSED enum with a keep-safe default:
  ATTRIBUTION (`<closed-child-id>` | none; STRUCTURAL for G3/G4 via `_container_child`, LLM+validated
  otherwise), CONTAINMENT (`limited-to-closed` | `spans-open-or-system` | `n-a`), LAYER
  (`plan-semantics` | `delivered-functionality` | `n-a`).
- Deterministic Pass-3 "completion floor" (third floor beside novelty ADR 0008 + drift-refresh
  ADR 0002) DROPS iff ALL: attribution=delivered-now child AND containment=limited-to-closed AND
  layer=plan-semantics AND priority<floor AND criterion NOT in the config-backed preserve-set
  (default T5c security / T10 contract). Every ambiguous value fails toward KEEP. Drops recorded to
  the REVIEW_RESULT sidecar with a distinct `drop_reason="completion"`; recomputed every run (a
  reopened/un-delivered child auto-resurfaces).
- `delivered-now`(child) = closed + VALID completion attestation (reuse the
  `compute_validity(...,'completion-verifier')` validity-on-read call — NOT the deficit-detector
  `_child_closure_findings` itself) OR superseded by a live in-epic SIBLING (structured `supersedes`
  link; direction B superseded_by A; A delivered-now or open/in_progress). Force-closed / abandoned /
  in-progress children are NOT delivered -> stay in scope. A parent reopen does NOT un-deliver
  children (ADR 0009 keys on each ticket's own last_reopened_at).

KEY EDGE DECISIONS: in-progress children stay FULLY in scope (v1). Force-close never trusted
(closes the laundering hole; the security/contract preserve-set is never dropped regardless of a
supersede edge). Sibling-supersede IN v1 (prevents the stale-closed-ticket hard-block Joe has
observed), sibling-verified. DEFERRED to separate epics: partial-completion attestation for
in-progress children; a structured `resolution` enum on `close`; the sidecar re-injection queue; an
optional second LAYER binary (calibration-gated).

PRIOR ART (why this is sound + why it is novel): NO prior art for scoped plan-quality review of a
partially-executed plan (LangGraph replan = regeneration not review; spec-kit /analyze =
completion-blind; incremental/delta verification = SAT not LLM) — benign gap (mainstream plans are
throwaway; rebar's signed, durable, verified-closed hierarchy is unusual). Suppression design
grounded in Tricorder/SonarQube/GitHub code-scanning (suppress on delivered+below-severity+
non-actionable, SEVERITY/SECURITY HARD VETO, never attribution-alone, never deletion); G-Eval/
CheckList/FLASK (few frozen atomic binaries + deterministic aggregation); ODC + IEEE-1028 (once
delivered, governing review shifts plan-review -> code-review; plan-semantics feedback goes moot,
delivered-substance stays live).

DECOMPOSITION (4 stories under 66ac, with file-impact + verify-commands + ordering deps):
- 6533 (reedy-hew-plume) Pass-3 completion floor + config evidence-gate + sidecar drop_reason + ADR 0020.
- 457a (tied-bulb-swoop) delivered-now predicate (validity-on-read reuse + sibling-supersede + reopen semantics).
- 94fd (tow-amok-jug) plan_review_completion Pass-2 sub-call + closed-enum contract + manifest assembler + degrade path.  depends_on 457a.
- 77cf (rag-yoke-clump) calibration gold-set (>=25 cases) + partially-complete-epic tests + reopen-resurface + close-op non-regression.  depends_on 6533, 94fd.
Ordering: 6533 depends_on 94fd + 457a; 77cf depends_on 6533 + 94fd; 94fd depends_on 457a.

OUTCOME: epic + 4 stories pass check-ac + clarity (7/5). `rebar review-plan 66ac` = PASS, blocking=0,
signed plan-review attestation certified at HEAD 49ad69b. Remediated across 4 rounds: 2 BLOCKs (name
the supersede artifact; fully enumerate the three sub-answer enums), all criticals (a real
`limited` vs `limited-to-closed` value-mismatch bug between 6533/94fd; the `_child_closure_findings`
deficit-detector inversion; missing sub-call output contract), and substantive majors (degrade path,
manifest owner, supersede signing rationale, determinism of attribution, reuse-vs-parallel, ADR 0020
+ cross-refs to 0008/0002, prompt-freeze-before-calibration, close-op non-regression). Residual: 5
sev=minor terminology/consistency advisories (severity-vs-priority wording, ADR-in-Scope, REVIEW_RESULT
naming) — triaged as diminishing-returns polish, not remediated further. NOT claimed/implemented —
this is the reviewed plan, ready to claim per-story.
